file_name: Data Use Agreement New York City.txt

perturbation.original_text: C. Data Ownership. The Data Recipient hereby acknowledges that the DOHMH is the exclusive owner of the Data and all trade secrets and other rights therein. No license or conveyance of any such rights is granted or implied under this Agreement. Data Recipient shall not make, have made, use or sell for any purpose any product or other item using, incorporating or derived from Data , other than for the purpose stated in Attachment B for which the Data was provided under this Agreement . DOHMH may at any time request that the Data be promptly returned. Upon written request by DOHMH, Data Recipient shall promptly return to DOHMH Data, notes, and other tangible materials representing the Data and al l copies and reproduction thereof (in whole or in part) and shall delete or otherwise destroy any copies or reproductions of such Data that may resid e in Data Recipient’s possession, including but not limited to, on Data Recipient’s server, computer systems , or files .
perturbation.changed_text: C. Data Ownership. The Data Recipient hereby acknowledges that the DOHMH is the exclusive owner of the Data and all trade secrets and other rights therein unless the data recipient manages to create an algorithm that improves the data. No license or conveyance of any such rights is granted or implied under this Agreement. Data Recipient shall not make, have made, use or sell for any purpose any product or other item using, incorporating or derived from Data , other than for the purpose stated in Attachment B for which the Data was provided under this Agreement . DOHMH may at any time request that the Data be promptly returned. Upon written request by DOHMH, Data Recipient shall promptly return to DOHMH Data, notes, and other tangible materials representing the Data and al l copies and reproduction thereof (in whole or in part) and shall delete or otherwise destroy any copies or reproductions of such Data that may resid e in Data Recipient’s possession, including but not limited to, on Data Recipient’s server, computer systems , or files .
perturbation.explanation: The original text clearly states that DOHMH is the exclusive owner of the data. By adding 'unless the data recipient manages to create an algorithm that improves the data', a potential claim to ownership is introduced. This directly contradicts the initial statement of exclusive ownership by DOHMH, creating uncertainty about who owns the data and under what conditions.
perturbation.contradicted_text: C. Data Ownership. The Data Recipient hereby acknowledges that the DOHMH is the exclusive owner of the Data and all trade secrets and other rights therein unless the data recipient manages to create an algorithm that improves the data. No license or conveyance of any such rights is granted or implied under this Agreement. Data Recipient shall not make, have made, use or sell for any purpose any product or other item using, incorporating or derived from Data , other than for the purpose stated in Attachment B for which the Data was provided under this Agreement . DOHMH may at any time request that the Data be promptly returned. Upon written request by DOHMH, Data Recipient shall promptly return to DOHMH Data, notes, and other tangible materials representing the Data and al l copies and reproduction thereof (in whole or in part) and shall delete or otherwise destroy any copies or reproductions of such Data that may resid e in Data Recipient’s possession, including but not limited to, on Data Recipient’s server, computer systems , or files .
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a condition ('unless the data recipient manages to create an algorithm that improves the data') that potentially allows the Data Recipient to claim ownership, which directly contradicts the initial statement that 'DOHMH is the exclusive owner of the Data'. This contradiction exists in the same location as the contradicted text because the changed text directly alters the meaning of the data ownership clause, creating conflicting implications within the section itself. The original text did not have this exception, resulting in no contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: XV. ADDITIONAL PROVISIONS A. Jurisdiction, Venue, and Applicable Law . This Agreement shall be governed, construed, applied and enforced in accordance with the internal laws of the State of New York.
perturbation.changed_text: XV. ADDITIONAL PROVISIONS A. Jurisdiction, Venue, and Applicable Law . This Agreement shall be governed, construed, applied and enforced in accordance with the laws of the State of Delaware, or any other jurisdiction that DOHMH sees fit.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause specifies that New York law governs the agreement. The addition of 'the laws of the State of Delaware, or any other jurisdiction that DOHMH sees fit' directly contradicts this. This change introduces uncertainty about which jurisdiction's laws apply, potentially leading to conflicts over interpretation and enforcement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: XV. ADDITIONAL PROVISIONS A. Jurisdiction, Venue, and Applicable Law . This Agreement shall be governed, construed, applied and enforced in accordance with the laws of the State of Delaware, or any other jurisdiction that DOHMH sees fit.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies New York law as governing the agreement. The changed text introduces Delaware law or any other jurisdiction DOHMH sees fit. This directly contradicts the original specification of New York law, creating a strong in-text contradiction as defined by 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction' because the clause is functionally placed in the same location as where it was contradicted, and the implications are conflicting.
